seo.title: BMI Calculator — Body Mass Index with Interpretation
seo.description: Calculate your BMI instantly. Get WHO category, healthy weight range, BMI Prime, and AI-powered health interpretation — free, fast, and accurate.

acceptedAnswer.text: BMI (Body Mass Index) is calculated from your height and weight. Formula: BMI = weight (kg) ÷ height² (m²). WHO defines four categories: Underweight (<18.5), Normal (18.5–24.9), Overweight (25–29.9), and Obese (≥30).
According to the WHO, a healthy BMI for adults is between 18.5 and 24.9.
BMI is a useful screening tool but has limitations. It does not directly measure body fat and does not account for muscle mass, bone density, or fat distribution.
BMI Prime is your BMI divided by 25. A value of 1.0 means you are at the upper limit of normal weight. Below 1.0 is normal or underweight; above 1.0 is overweight or obese.
The Ponderal Index is weight (kg) ÷ height³ (m³). It scales better than BMI for very tall or short individuals. Normal range is approximately 11–14 kg/m³.

faq.a2: According to the WHO, a healthy BMI for adults is between 18.5 and 24.9.
faq.a3: BMI is a useful screening tool but has limitations. It does not directly measure body fat and does not account for muscle mass, bone density, or fat distribution.
faq.a4: BMI Prime is your BMI divided by 25. A value of 1.0 means you are at the upper limit of normal weight. Below 1.0 is normal or underweight; above 1.0 is overweight or obese.
faq.a5: The Ponderal Index is weight (kg) ÷ height³ (m³). It scales better than BMI for very tall or short individuals. Normal range is approximately 11–14 kg/m³.

aria.form_description: Enter your height and weight to calculate your Body Mass Index.

inputs.name: age
inputs.description: Age in years (used for Deurenberg body fat estimation)

inputs.name: sex
inputs.description: Biological sex (used for Deurenberg body fat estimation)

outputs.name: bmiPrime
outputs.description: BMI Prime (ratio to upper limit of normal, 25)

outputs.name: healthyWeightMin
outputs.description: Lower bound of healthy weight range for this height

outputs.name: healthyWeightMax
outputs.description: Upper bound of healthy weight range for this height

outputs.name: bodyFatPercentage
outputs.description: Estimated body fat % via Deurenberg formula (requires age + sex)
